description.task: Implement an InfixToPostfix class that converts an infix expression to postfix (Reverse Polish Notation) using a stack. Handle operators +, -, *, / with standard precedence (* and / higher than + and -).
instructions: On ')': while stack.peek()!='(' append stack.pop() to result; then pop the '(' (discard it)
On operator: while stack is not empty && stack.peek()!='(' && precedence(stack.peek())>=precedence(c): pop and append to result; push c
After the main loop: pop all remaining operators from stack and append to result
A+B*C → ABC*+ (B*C has higher precedence, evaluated first)
description.hint: operator case: while(!stack.isEmpty()&&stack.peek()!='('&&precedence(stack.peek())>=precedence(c)) result.append(stack.pop()); stack.push(c); After loop: while(!stack.isEmpty()) result.append(stack.pop());

description.task: Implement a PostfixEvaluator class that evaluates a postfix (RPN) expression given as a space-separated string. Operands are pushed onto a stack; operators pop two operands, compute, and push the result.
instructions: When token is an operator: int b=stack.pop(), a=stack.pop(); compute a op b; push result
Note the order: first popped is the right operand (b), second popped is the left operand (a)
The regex '-?\\d+' matches both positive and negative integers

description.task: Implement a MazeSolver class that uses an explicit stack (not recursion) to find a path from start (0,0) to end (n-1,n-1) in a 2D grid. 0=open, 1=wall. Track the path using a stack of int[] {row, col}.
instructions: For each direction (right, down, left, up), compute nr=curr[0]+dir[0], nc=curr[1]+dir[1]
If valid: visited[nr][nc]=true; stack.push({nr,nc}); create newPath=new ArrayList<>(path); newPath.add({nr,nc}); pathStack.push(newPath)
The maze leads from (0,0) to (3,3) through a valid 7-step path
description.hint: for(int[] d:dirs){int nr=curr[0]+d[0],nc=curr[1]+d[1]; if(nr>=0&&nr<n&&nc>=0&&nc<n&&maze[nr][nc]==0&&!visited[nr][nc]){visited[nr][nc]=true; stack.push(new int[]{nr,nc}); List<int[]> np=new ArrayList<>(path); np.add(new int[]{nr,nc}); pathStack.push(np);}}
